source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The output voltage of an inverting converter circuit is a function of the input voltage and the duty cycle of the switching signal, represented by the variable D (ranging in value from 0% to 100%), where \( D = \frac{t_{on}}{t_{on} + t_{off}} \). Based on this mathematical relationship, calculate the output voltage of this converter circuit at a duty cycle D=100% , assuming an input voltage of 40 volts. The output voltage is ______V.
